I see. The shapes moved down significantly:
- Black L: rows 36-44 (originally 15-23), cols 21-29

Also the dark gray L has 45 pixels again - it seems like the merge was undone when I moved LEFT. Let me check...

- So the merge was indeed reversed when I moved LEFT
So the shapes can merge and un-merge. The merge happens when they overlap, and the un-merge happens when they separate. This means the merge is reversible.
Now the black L is at rows 36-44, and the yellow L is at rows 45-53. They're 1 row apart. If I move DOWN once more, the black L would be at rows 39-47, overlapping with the yellow L at rows 45-47.
But wait - the black L is in the LEFT panel (cols 21-29) and the yellow L is in the RIGHT panel (cols 51-59). They're in different panels, so they can't overlap directly.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics:
- LEFT/RIGHT move the two top shapes toward/away from each other horizontally
- UP/DOWN move both top shapes vertically
- The yellow L at bottom right doesn't move
Maybe the goal is to:

2. The black L (left) serves as a guide/template with the white dots

- Yellow L: rows 45-53, cols 51-59

- But UP/DOWN moves BOTH top shapes, and LEFT/RIGHT moves them in opposite directions
Actually, I think I've been misunderstanding the movement. Let me re-examine:
- When I pressed LEFT, the left shape moved left (cols 18→15) and the right shape moved right (cols 36→39)
- When I pressed RIGHT, the left shape moved right and the right shape moved left
- So they move in opposite directions horizontally
To move the dark gray L to the right (toward the yellow L), I need to press LEFT (which moves the right shape further right).
To move it down, I need to press DOWN.
